skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Runs web searches through a local Gemini CLI wrapper for current research, documentation lookup, troubleshooting, and comparative analysis.
skill.summary: Search the web with Gemini CLI and return cited research summaries.
skill.author: Cain96

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are mostly documentation examples, but the underlying behavior is real: the skill runs a local Gemini CLI through Bash. The weak-cryptography findings are false positives in prose, while the confirmed high-risk issue is the --yolo auto-approval flag combined with user-controlled search prompts.

high_findings.title: External Gemini CLI Runs With Auto-Approval
high_findings.description: The shell wrapper sends the user query to the Gemini CLI and invokes it with --yolo. This is a high-risk pattern because auto-approval can allow the external AI CLI to perform enabled actions without per-action user review, depending on the local Gemini configuration.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly calls gemini with user-controlled prompt text and the --yolo flag. The exact enabled Gemini tools depend on local configuration, but the auto-approval behavior is explicit in the command.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Based Search Workflow Requires External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill documentation directs users to run a Bash script for searches. This is an intentional feature, but it increases operational risk because results and behavior depend on an installed external CLI and its credentials, network access, and permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited documentation repeatedly instructs execution of the search script through Bash. This confirms external command use, although the examples themselves are documentation rather than executable code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static detections for weak cryptography point to prose and help text, not cryptographic functions or algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic implementation in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain descriptions, expected-output bullets, or usage text. They do not contain cryptographic API calls or algorithm names used in code.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Gemini CLI Runs With Auto-Approval
dangerous_patterns.description: The shell wrapper sends the user query to the Gemini CLI and invokes it with --yolo. This is a high-risk pattern because auto-approval can allow the external AI CLI to perform enabled actions without per-action user review, depending on the local Gemini configuration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script directly calls gemini with user-controlled prompt text and the --yolo flag. The exact enabled Gemini tools depend on local configuration, but the auto-approval behavior is explicit in the command.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash-Based Search Workflow Requires External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documentation directs users to run a Bash script for searches. This is an intentional feature, but it increases operational risk because results and behavior depend on an installed external CLI and its credentials, network access, and permissions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited documentation repeatedly instructs execution of the search script through Bash. This confirms external command use, although the examples themselves are documentation rather than executable code.

content.user_title: Search the Web With Gemini CLI
content.value_statement: Current technical research often needs sources beyond the local project. This skill runs a Gemini CLI search workflow and asks for cited Markdown answers.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Bash wrapper that calls the local gemini command with a search prompt.
Requests current web information and cited source URLs from Gemini CLI.
Supports natural-language research questions about technical topics and news.
Provides examples for documentation lookup, debugging, comparisons, and migration planning.
Asks Gemini to return Markdown-formatted answers with code examples when relevant.
limitations: Requires the gemini command to be installed, configured, and authenticated locally.
Uses external network search and depends on Gemini CLI availability and permissions.
The script uses --yolo, which should be reviewed before marketplace publication.
Search quality and source accuracy depend on Gemini output and user verification.

use_cases.title: Research Recent Framework Changes
use_cases.description: Find recent release notes, migration guidance, and examples for frameworks such as Next.js, React, or TypeScript.

use_cases.title: Investigate Error Messages
use_cases.description: Search community discussions and documentation for likely causes, fixes, and related issue reports.

use_cases.title: Compare Technical Options
use_cases.description: Collect cited comparisons, trade-offs, and adoption signals before choosing libraries, tools, or architecture patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Current Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a recent answer with source links.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for current information about [topic]. Summarize the key facts and include source URLs.

prompt_templates.title: Documentation Lookup
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when official documentation should be prioritized.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find official documentation for [library or tool] about [feature]. Explain the recommended usage and cite the source pages.

prompt_templates.title: Error Investigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when debugging an unfamiliar message.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for this error: [exact error]. Find likely causes, verified fixes, and relevant issue discussions with links.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Research Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when choosing between technical options.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare [option A], [option B], and [option C] for [use case]. Include advantages, disadvantages, risks, and source URLs.

output_examples.input: Search for the latest TypeScript release changes and migration notes.
output_examples.output: A concise research summary listing major changes, compatibility notes, migration concerns, and links to official release material.

output_examples.input: Compare Vite and Webpack for a large React application.
output_examples.output: A structured comparison covering build speed, plugin ecosystem, configuration complexity, migration effort, and cited references.

output_examples.input: Find causes and fixes for a React undefined map error.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting answer with common causes, practical fixes, prevention tips, and links to documentation or issue discussions.

best_practices: Use precise queries that name the product, version, error text, or decision criteria.
Ask for official sources when accuracy matters, then cross-check important claims.
Review the Gemini CLI configuration and permissions before running the Bash wrapper.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for information already available in the local repository.
Do not treat search results as authoritative without checking the cited sources.
Do not run the script with sensitive project context unless external disclosure is acceptable.

faq.question: Does this skill perform search by itself?
faq.answer: No. It calls the local gemini command through a Bash script and asks Gemini to search the web.

faq.question: What tools must be installed?
faq.answer: The Gemini CLI must be installed, configured, and authenticated before the script can return search results.

faq.question: Why is this audit marked high risk?
faq.answer: The script uses --yolo with an external AI CLI, which can bypass action-by-action approval depending on local settings.

faq.question: Are the weak cryptography warnings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The cited lines contain prose and examples, not weak cryptographic code.

faq.question: Can it cite sources?
faq.answer: The prompt asks Gemini to include source URLs, but users should verify important references manually.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Queries are sent to an external CLI workflow and may expose sensitive context to external services.
